requires login.reason: The `run:` step at line 49 directly interpolates the user-supplied `inputs.model` (a `workflow_dispatch` input, controlled by whoever triggers the workflow) into the shell script via `${{ inputs.model }}`. Because GitHub Actions evaluates `${{ ... }}` before the shell parses the script, an attacker who sets `model` to a value containing shell metacharacters (e.g., `; rm -rf $HOME`) would have those characters interpreted as shell syntax, not as literal data. The same workflow demonstrates the safe pattern at lines 41-42, where `inputs.base_url` is passed through an environment variable instead of being interpolated directly. The action's own code (`action.yml:174-175`) confirms awareness: 'Inputs reach the shell only via env (never interpolated into the script), so a hostile model/base-url string cannot inject commands.' That safety property is violated here for `inputs.model`.
